description: Professional design career library covering major creative and design disciplines.

careers.name: Graphic Designer
careers.overview: Graphic Designer is a creative profession focused on innovation, aesthetics, problem solving and user-centered design.
careers.whyChoose: Excellent creative freedom, strong portfolio-based growth and opportunities across industries.
careers.eligibility: 10+2 from any stream. Design aptitude and portfolio development are beneficial.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 → B.Des/BFA/Design Degree → Internship → Professional Practice or Higher Studies.
careers.workEnvironment: Design studios, agencies, startups, brands, technology companies and freelance practice.
careers.futureScope: Strong demand due to digital products, branding, innovation and experience-driven businesses.
careers.aiImpact: AI assists with creative workflows but human creativity, research and design thinking remain critical.

careers.name: Communication Designer
careers.overview: Communication Designer is a creative profession focused on innovation, aesthetics, problem solving and user-centered design.
careers.whyChoose: Excellent creative freedom, strong portfolio-based growth and opportunities across industries.
careers.eligibility: 10+2 from any stream. Design aptitude and portfolio development are beneficial.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 → B.Des/BFA/Design Degree → Internship → Professional Practice or Higher Studies.
careers.workEnvironment: Design studios, agencies, startups, brands, technology companies and freelance practice.
careers.futureScope: Strong demand due to digital products, branding, innovation and experience-driven businesses.
careers.aiImpact: AI assists with creative workflows but human creativity, research and design thinking remain critical.

careers.name: UI Designer
careers.overview: UI Designer is a creative career focused on solving user, visual, product or communication problems through design thinking.
careers.whyChoose: UI Designer offers creative expression, growing demand in digital products and opportunities across many industries.
careers.eligibility: Class 12 in any stream; portfolio and entrance exams may be required.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 -> Design entrance or portfolio -> Design degree/diploma -> Internship -> Professional practice.
careers.workEnvironment: Design studios, product companies, agencies, media firms, fashion houses, startups and freelance practice.
careers.futureScope: Strong due to digital products, brand experience, user experience and creative technology.
careers.aiImpact: AI will speed up ideation and production, while strategy, taste, research and original thinking become more valuable.

careers.name: UX Designer
careers.overview: Designs user experiences for digital products.
careers.whyChoose: High demand and excellent growth.
careers.eligibility: 10+2 any stream
careers.educationPath: B.Des/BFA/UX courses
careers.workEnvironment: Tech companies and design studios
careers.futureScope: Excellent
careers.aiImpact: AI assists but does not replace user-centered design

careers.name: UX Researcher
careers.overview: UX Researcher works in a specialized professional pathway with applied knowledge, problem solving and industry-ready skills.
careers.whyChoose: UX Researcher is useful because it offers focused career growth, practical skill building and opportunities across relevant organizations.
careers.eligibility: Class 12 or graduation requirements vary by institution and specialization.
careers.educationPath: Class 12 -> Relevant degree/diploma/certification -> Internship/projects -> Professional role.
careers.workEnvironment: Companies, institutions, agencies, startups, government bodies and specialist teams.
careers.futureScope: Strong for students who build practical skills, portfolios, internships and updated domain knowledge.
careers.aiImpact: AI will support routine work while judgement, communication and specialist expertise remain important.
cons: Early career may need portfolio or training
